extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won Paris sprint by leading out from 400m, beating sprinters who waited behind him expecting a fade.
tactical_patterns: GROENEWEGEN Dylan launched from 400m out, unusually long sprint distance that forced followers into headwind disadvantage.
GUSEV Dimitri's late attack at 2km forced sprint teams into premature chase, disrupting lead-out train organization.
Quickstep STENAR attempted counterattack inside 5km, secondary disruption tactic preventing clean sprint setup.

rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: first Grand Tour victory demonstrates willingness to take sprint risk from distance rather than wait for positioning
rider_signals.KRISTOFF Alexander: negative: expected favorite failed to cover 400m launch, suggesting poor positioning or legs
rider_signals.GUSEV Dimitri: neutral: 2km solo attack timing reveals understanding that late disruptions fragment sprint trains more effectively than early breaks
